facts.label: A SPOT NOBODY WANTED
facts.story: Cuba only reached France 1938 because almost nobody else showed up. Uruguay and Argentina boycotted over the host decision, Spain was mid-civil war, and every CONCACAF rival except Cuba withdrew rather than cross the Atlantic. Nineteen amateur players, most from Havana clubs and none of whom had ever left the island, inherited a place by walkover — then went out and beat Romania anyway.

facts.label: THE BACKUP WHO HELD THE FORT
facts.story: When Benito Carvajales couldn't go again for the Romania replay, Cuba turned to Juan Ayra, a reserve goalkeeper from CD Centro Gallego. Romania had already forced extra time once and taken a first-half lead again in the replay, but Ayra stood firm behind a defense that somehow protected a one-goal cushion, sending an amateur Caribbean side into the quarter-finals of the World Cup in its very first appearance.

annotations.label: TUÑAS SAVES THE DAY
annotations.story: Cuba twice fought back from behind against Romania in Toulouse. Héctor Socorro leveled it at 1–1, Tomás Fernández put Cuba ahead late, and after Romania equalized again in extra time, Juan Tuñas struck with three minutes left to force a 3–3 draw and a replay.

annotations.label: THE REPLAY WIN
annotations.story: Four days later at the same Toulouse stadium, Romania led at the break again. But Cuba erupted early in the second half — Socorro equalized, then Carlos Oliveira struck the winner six minutes later — sending the debutants through 2–1 and into the quarter-finals.

annotations.label: THE CRASH BACK TO EARTH
annotations.story: Exhausted from two matches with Romania in five days, Cuba was overrun by Sweden's Tore Keller and Gustav Wetterström, who both scored hat-tricks in an 8–0 rout — still Cuba's heaviest defeat in team history. Tomás Fernández even missed a penalty as the rout wore on.
